<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Waiting For implementation ready for review">
The Waiting For feature implementation (Step 8 / US-GTD-5) is complete and all pipeline checks pass. Here is a summary for review:

## Implementation Summary

### Domain Layer (`src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs`)
- `WaitingForId(Uuid)` — newtype wrapper
- `WaitingOnPerson` — non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed; constructed via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`
- `WaitingForItem` enum — `Active` / `Completed` variants (same state machine pattern as NextAction/Project)
- 15 unit tests covering construction, validation, state transitions

### Infrastructure Layer (`src/infrastructure/waiting_for_repository.rs`)
- `insert_waiting_for_item`, `find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user`, `find_waiting_for_item_by_id`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Migration: `waiting_for_items` table with id, user_id (FK), title, waiting_on, created_at, completed_at

### Service Layer (`src/services/waiting_for_service.rs`)
- `add_waiting_for_item`, `list_active_waiting_for_items`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Error types: `AddWaitingForError`, `CompleteWaitingForError`, `DeleteWaitingForError`, `UpdateWaitingForError`
- All service functions have tracing instrumentation

### Routes (`src/routes/waiting_for.rs`)
- GET /waiting-for — list active items
- POST /waiting-for — add new item (title + waiting_on person)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete — mark as received
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/delete — delete item
- GET /waiting-for/{id}/edit — get inline edit form (HTMX)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/edit — update item
- GET /waiting-for/{id} — get single item fragment (for cancel edit)
- Full HTMX support with HX-Trigger announcements

### Templates
- `waiting_for.html` — main page with two-field add form ("What are you waiting for?" + "Who or what?"), empty state, semantic list
- `waiting_for_item.html` — item partial with Edit link, Received button, Delete button, all with aria-labels
- `waiting_for_edit.html` — inline edit form with title + waiting_on inputs, Save/Cancel, visually hidden labels

### Dashboard Integration
- Dynamic waiting_for_count displayed on dashboard stat card

### CSS (`static/css/main.css`)
- ~150 lines of waiting-for component CSS in @layer components
- Follows existing patterns (similar to projects, next-actions)

### Focus Management JS (`static/js/waiting-for-focus.js`)
- WCAG 2.4.3 compliant focus management for HTMX swaps
- Handles delete/complete (focus next/prev sibling), edit mode, empty state transitions

### Tests
- 85 unit tests (15 new for WaitingForItem domain)
- 220 integration tests (30 new for waiting-for routes)
- 11 Playwright e2e tests (all pass, no regression)

### Glossary (`docs/glossary.md`)
- Updated with WaitingForItem, WaitingForId, WaitingOnPerson types
- Added waiting-for actions, error types, state machine diagram, error copy convention

### Pipeline Status
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: PASS
- `cargo test`: PASS (305 total)
- `npx playwright test`: PASS (11 e2e)

Please review and provide feedback. I am ready to commit once we have consensus.
</teammate-message>

1→# Domain Glossary
     2→
     3→Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
     4→Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
     5→says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.
     6→
     7→Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Core Domain Types
    12→
    13→| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
    14→|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
    15→| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
    16→| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
    17→| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
    18→| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
    19→| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
    20→| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
    21→| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
    22→| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
    23→| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |
    24→
    25→## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
    26→
    27→| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
    28→|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
    29→| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
    30→| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
    31→| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
    32→| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
    33→| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
    34→| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
    35→| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
    36→| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
    37→| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
    38→| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |
    39→
    40→## Domain Errors
    41→
    42→| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
    43→|-----------------|-----------|-------|
    44→| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
    45→| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
    46→| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    47→| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    48→| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    49→| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    50→| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
    51→| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
    52→| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
    53→| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
    54→| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    55→| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    56→| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
    57→| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
    58→| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
    59→| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
    60→| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    61→| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    62→| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    63→| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    64→| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    65→| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    66→| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |
    67→| Inbox item not found (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
    68→| Not authorized (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
    69→| Invalid title (add project) | `AddProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_project service |
    70→| Project not found (complete) | `CompleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
    71→| Not authorized (complete project) | `CompleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
    72→| Project not found (delete) | `DeleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
    73→| Not authorized (delete project) | `DeleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
    74→| Project not found (edit) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
    75→| Not authorized (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
    76→| Invalid title (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_project_title service |
    77→| Project not found (add action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::ProjectNotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
    78→| Not authorized (add project action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
    79→| Inbox item not found (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
    80→| Not authorized (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
    81→| Invalid first action title (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | First action title validation failure in clarify_as_project service |
    82→| Waiting-on person empty | `WaitingOnPersonError::Empty` | Waiting-on person cannot be blank |
    83→| Waiting-on person too long | `WaitingOnPersonError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Waiting-on person exceeds 100 character limit |
    84→| Invalid title (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
    85→| Invalid person (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnPersonError)` | Person validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
    86→| Waiting-for not found (complete) | `CompleteWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
    87→| Not authorized (complete waiting-for) | `CompleteWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
    88→| Waiting-for not found (delete) | `DeleteWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
    89→| Not authorized (delete waiting-for) | `DeleteWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
    90→| Waiting-for not found (edit) | `UpdateWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
    91→| Not authorized (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
    92→| Invalid title (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |
    93→| Invalid person (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnPersonError)` | Person validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |
    94→
    95→## Error Copy Convention
    96→
    97→Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
    98→are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
    99→tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.
   100→
   101→The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
   102→call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.
   103→
   104→| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
   105→|-------------|-----------------|
   106→| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
   107→| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
   108→| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
   109→| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
   110→| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
   111→| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
   112→| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
   113→| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
   114→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
   115→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
   116→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
   117→
   118→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
   119→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
   120→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
   121→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add waiting-for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
   122→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit waiting-for) | "Title cannot be empty" |
   123→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (waiting-for) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
   124→| `WaitingOnPersonError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
   125→| `WaitingOnPersonError::Empty` (edit) | "Who or what cannot be empty" |
   126→| `WaitingOnPersonError::TooLong` | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |
   127→
   128→*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*
   129→
   130→---
   131→
   132→## Type Design Principles
   133→
   134→These principles govern how we model the domain:
   135→
   136→1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   137→   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   138→   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.
   139→
   140→2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   141→   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   142→   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   143→   inconsistent.
   144→
   145→3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   146→   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   147→   abstractions in Rust.
   148→
   149→4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   150→   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   151→   I/O has leaked into the domain.
   152→
   153→5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   154→   ```
   155→   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   156→   ```
   157→   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.
   158→
   159→## State Machine: TodoItem
   160→
   161→```
   162→                 add_todo()
   163→  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
   164→                                 │  ^
   165→               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
   166→                                 │  │
   167→                                 v  │
   168→                             Completed
   169→```
   170→
   171→Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
   172→The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
   173→Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).
   174→
   175→---
   176→
   177→## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)
   178→
   179→*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
   180→team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
   181→implementation.*
   182→
   183→### Core GTD Concepts
   184→
   185→| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
   186→|-------------|-------------------|-------|
   187→| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
   188→| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
   189→| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
   190→| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. **Implemented.** |
   191→| Project ID | `ProjectId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a project. **Implemented.** |
   192→| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
   193→| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
   194→| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` (enum: Active, Completed) | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (person), and date added. **Implemented.** |
   195→| Waiting For item ID | `WaitingForId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a waiting-for item. **Implemented.** |
   196→| Waiting-on person | `WaitingOnPerson` | Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. The person or entity the user is waiting on. Validated at construction via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
   197→| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
   198→| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
   199→| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
   200→
   201→### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
   202→
   203→| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
   204→|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
   205→| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
   206→| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
   207→| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction. **Implemented.** |
   208→| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem (not yet implemented as clarify action) |
   209→| Add waiting-for item | `add_waiting_for_item()` | Service: creates WaitingForItem (Active) with title and waiting_on person. **Implemented.** |
   210→| Mark received (complete waiting-for) | `WaitingForItem::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   211→| Delete waiting-for item | `delete_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   212→| Edit waiting-for item | `update_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title and person, persists. **Implemented.** |
   213→| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
   214→| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
   215→| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
   216→| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   217→| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   218→| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
   219→| Add project | `add_project()` | Service: creates Project (Active) with title. **Implemented.** |
   220→| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   221→| Drop project | `Project::drop_project()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned. **Implemented.** |
   222→| Delete project | `delete_project()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   223→| Edit project title | `update_project_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
   224→| Add next action to project | `add_next_action_to_project()` | Service: creates NextAction linked to project. **Implemented.** |
   225→| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
   226→| Resolve waiting for | `complete_waiting_for_item()` | WaitingForItem -> Completed (marked as received). **Implemented.** |
   227→| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
   228→| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |
   229→
   230→### GTD State Machines
   231→
   232→**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
   233→```
   234→                capture()
   235→  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
   236→                                │
   237→                      clarify() │
   238→              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
   239→              v        v        v        v        v
   240→         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
   241→```
   242→
   243→**NextAction Lifecycle:**
   244→```
   245→                  clarify_as_next_action()
   246→  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
   247→                                               │
   248→                                   complete()  │
   249→                                               v
   250→                                         NextAction (Completed)
   251→```
   252→
   253→**Project Lifecycle:**
   254→```
   255→                    clarify_as_project()
   256→  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
   257→                                           │  │
   258→                              complete()   │  │  drop()
   259→                                           v  v
   260→                                    Completed / Dropped
   261→```
   262→
   263→**WaitingForItem Lifecycle:**
   264→```
   265→              add_waiting_for_item()
   266→  [nothing] ───────────────────────> WaitingForItem (Active)
   267→                                         │
   268→                             complete()  │  (mark as received)
   269→                                         v
   270→                                   WaitingForItem (Completed)
   271→```
   272→
   273→Both Active and Completed variants can be deleted (`delete_waiting_for_item()`).
   274→The `update_waiting_for_item()` action applies to Active items.
   275→

# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add waiting-for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit waiting-for) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (waiting-for) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `WaitingOnPersonError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `WaitingOnPersonError::Empty` (edit) | "Who or what cannot be empty" |
| `WaitingOnPersonError::TooLong` | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

---

## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)

*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
implementation.*

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. **Implemented.** |
| Project ID | `ProjectId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a project. **Implemented.** |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` (enum: Active, Completed) | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (person), and date added. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item ID | `WaitingForId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a waiting-for item. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting-on person | `WaitingOnPerson` | Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. The person or entity the user is waiting on. Validated at construction via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction. **Implemented.** |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem (not yet implemented as clarify action) |
| Add waiting-for item | `add_waiting_for_item()` | Service: creates WaitingForItem (Active) with title and waiting_on person. **Implemented.** |
| Mark received (complete waiting-for) | `WaitingForItem::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete waiting-for item | `delete_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit waiting-for item | `update_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title and person, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add project | `add_project()` | Service: creates Project (Active) with title. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Drop project | `Project::drop_project()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned. **Implemented.** |
| Delete project | `delete_project()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit project title | `update_project_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action to project | `add_next_action_to_project()` | Service: creates NextAction linked to project. **Implemented.** |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `complete_waiting_for_item()` | WaitingForItem -> Completed (marked as received). **Implemented.** |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped
```

**WaitingForItem Lifecycle:**
```
              add_waiting_for_item()
  [nothing] ───────────────────────> WaitingForItem (Active)
                                         │
                             complete()  │  (mark as received)
                                         v
                                   WaitingForItem (Completed)
```

Both Active and Completed variants can be deleted (`delete_waiting_for_item()`).
The `update_waiting_for_item()` action applies to Active items.

    12→    created_at: DateTime<Utc>,

    16→impl WaitingForRecord {
    17→    fn into_domain(self) -> WaitingForItem {
    18→        let id = WaitingForId::from_uuid(self.id);
    19→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    20→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");

    31→        )

    35→pub async fn insert_waiting_for_item(
    36→    pool: &PgPool,
    37→    item: &WaitingForItem,

    40→        WaitingForItem::Completed { completed_at, .. } => Some(completed_at),
    41→        WaitingForItem::Active { .. } => None,

    44→    sqlx::query!(
    45→        r#"INSERT INTO waiting_for_items (id, user_id, title, waiting_on, created_at, completed_at)
    46→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5 :: timestamptz, $6 :: timestamptz)"#,
    47→        item.id().as_uuid(),
    48→        item.user_id().as_uuid(),
    49→        item.title().as_str(),
    50→        item.waiting_on().as_str(),
    51→        item.created_at() as &DateTime<Utc>,
    52→        completed_at as Option<&DateTime<Utc>>,

input.content:
Scott Wlaschin here with my domain architecture review of the Step 8 implementation.

I've read: `src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs`, `src/domain/mod.rs`, `src/services/waiting_for_service.rs`, `src/infrastructure/waiting_for_repository.rs`, the migration, `docs/glossary.md`, and `docs/deferred-items.md`.

---

### BLOCKING

**1. Naming: `WaitingOnPerson` should be `WaitingOn`, and `WaitingOnPersonError` should be `WaitingOnError`**

I raised this in my pre-implementation review and it was not addressed. The glossary and user story say "who or what" -- it could be a person, a vendor, a delivery, an event ("insurance approval"), a shipment, a build system. `WaitingOnPerson` implies only people. The DB column is simply `waiting_on` and the user story says "who/what it's waiting on." The domain concept is "what you're waiting on," not "what person you're waiting on."

This affects:
- `WaitingOnPerson` -> `WaitingOn` (struct name, `src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs:32`)
- `WaitingOnPersonError` -> `WaitingOnError` (error enum, `src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs:36-42`)
- Error messages: "Waiting-on person cannot be empty" -> "Waiting-on description cannot be empty"
- Error messages: "Waiting-on person too long" -> "Waiting-on description too long"
- All references in services, routes, glossary, error copy table
- Glossary entry at line 196: "Waiting-on person" -> "Waiting on" with description "Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. The person or entity the user is waiting on."

**2. Naming: `Completed` variant should be `Resolved`, method should be `resolve()`, field should be `resolved_at`**

I also raised this in pre-implementation review. The glossary's proposed schema uses `resolved_at`, and this is a fundamentally different domain concept from NextAction completion. When you receive what you were waiting for, you "resolve" the waiting-for item -- you don't "complete" it the way you complete an action. Using the same word for both creates ambiguity in the ubiquitous language.

This affects:
- `WaitingForItem::Completed` -> `WaitingForItem::Resolved` (variant name)
- `completed_at` field -> `resolved_at` (in domain type, DB column, migration, repository, service)
- `complete()` method -> `resolve()` (domain type method)
- `is_completed()` -> `is_resolved()` (accessor)
- `CompleteWaitingForError` -> `ResolveWaitingForError` (service error type)
- `complete_waiting_for_item()` service/repo functions -> `resolve_waiting_for_item()`
- Route path: `/waiting-for/{id}/complete` -> `/waiting-for/{id}/resolve`
- Glossary state machine diagram and all references
- The glossary currently says `complete_waiting_for_item()` on line 226 for "Resolve waiting for" -- this is internally inconsistent. The business action says "Resolve" but the function says "complete."

**3. Missing deferred item for project_id**

I asked in pre-implementation review that the `project_id` deferral be documented in `docs/deferred-items.md`. The data model sketch in `gtd-product-discovery.md` includes `project_id FK -> projects NULL` for `waiting_for_items`, but the implementation (correctly) skips it. This deferral needs to be tracked. Add an entry:

"Waiting For items do not yet support project linkage (project_id FK). Add when a user story requires it." | Domain | Step 8 (Scott) | Refinement | Open |

---

### Minor

**4. Glossary line 226 is self-contradictory**

The "Business Action" column says "Resolve waiting for" but the "Proposed Rust Function" column says `complete_waiting_for_item()`. This will become consistent once blocking item #2 is addressed, but I'm flagging it as an additional data point that the domain language expects "resolve," not "complete."

**5. Glossary state machine diagram uses `complete()`**

Line 268: `complete()  |  (mark as received)` -- should be `resolve()  |  (mark as received)` after fix.

---

### What's done well

- `WaitingForId` newtype follows the established pattern perfectly.
- The `WaitingOn*` type correctly uses parse/validate pattern with trimming.
- The enum state machine (`Active` / terminal state) follows the NextAction pattern.
- `from_parts` reconstruction is clean.
- Domain layer is pure -- no I/O imports, no infrastructure leakage.
- Service layer properly maps domain errors to service errors.
- Repository uses `into_domain()` pattern consistently.
- Error types are properly separated per operation.
- Unit tests cover all the right cases (construction, validation, state transitions, from_parts).
- Migration is clean and minimal.

The structural design is excellent. The three blocking issues are all naming/terminology -- the code structure itself is sound. Once the naming aligns with the ubiquitous language, this gets my approval.

content.text:
I've sent my detailed domain architecture review to Luca. Summary of my findings:

**3 BLOCKING items:**
1. **`WaitingOnPerson` -> `WaitingOn`** -- the domain concept covers any entity (person, vendor, delivery, event), not just people. Both the struct and error type need renaming.
2. **`Completed` -> `Resolved`** -- "resolving" a waiting-for item is a distinct domain concept from "completing" a next action. The glossary's proposed schema uses `resolved_at`. This requires renaming the variant, field, method (`resolve()`), accessor (`is_resolved()`), service error type, and route path.
3. **Missing deferred item** -- the `project_id` FK deferral needs to be tracked in `docs/deferred-items.md`.

**2 Minor items** (glossary inconsistencies that will be fixed by blocking item #2).

The structural design is excellent -- clean domain layer, proper newtypes, parse-don't-validate, enum state machine, pure domain core with no I/O leakage. The issues are all naming/terminology alignment with the ubiquitous language.
